task_completion.description: Google Earth is switched to Sky mode
task_completion.visual_evidence: The viewport background shows a starfield (black space with stars) rather than a globe, or the 'Sky' mode icon (often resembling Saturn) is highlighted in the toolbar.

task_completion.description: Agent searches for the target galaxy
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search sidebar shows a query for 'Andromeda Galaxy', 'M31', 'Messier 31', or 'NGC 224'.

task_completion.description: Andromeda Galaxy is centered in the viewport
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large spiral galaxy is clearly visible and centered in the main application window.

task_completion.description: The Save Image interface is activated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Image' toolbar is visible at the top of the viewport, showing options for 'Map Options', 'Resolution', and 'Save Image'.

task_completion.description: Resolution is set to at least 1920x1080
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Resolution dropdown in the Save Image toolbar displays '1920 x 1080', 'Maximum', or a setting higher than 1080p.

task_completion.description: File is saved with the correct path and name
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog is visible with the filename 'andromeda_m31.jpg' and the folder path ending in 'Documents', or a file manager window shows the newly created file at that location.

integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Google Earth Pro menus, toolbars, and search panels, not just terminal commands.
No evidence of using terminal commands like 'wget' or 'curl' to download a galaxy image from the internet.

integrity.description: Image is rendered by the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The galaxy view is inside the Google Earth Pro window frame, not a static image opened in an image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target object is the Andromeda Galaxy, also known as M31, Messier 31, or NGC 224. It appears as a spiral galaxy. The application must be in 'Sky' mode, characterized by a dark starfield background.
